examples: The different therapy was used for deferent parts of herpes zoster.
Shingles (herpes zoster virus) is a painful viral infection of the nerve roots.
Antiviral therapy is reserved for cases of varicella-zoster and herpes simplex infection.
Shingles (also called herpes zoster) occurs when a person's past chickenpox infection is reactivated.
Postherpetic neuralgia (post-her-PET-ic noo-RAL-jah) is a complication of shingles, which is caused by the chickenpox (herpes zoster) virus.
